description: A designed data-visualization card (White House branded). The top portion is a dramatic darkened photograph of President Donald Trump in a dark navy suit, white turtleneck/shirt and red tie with an American-flag pin, pointing directly at the viewer with a stern expression, set against a dim interior with warm overhead lights and what appears to be a dinosaur/whale skeleton (a museum setting). Centered below in large bold white serif all-caps is the headline 'AMERICANS FIRST.' Beneath that is a white panel containing a line chart titled 'Total Native-Born Employment Level' with a legend distinguishing 'Biden Administration' (blue line) and 'Trump Administration (so far)' (red line). The x-axis runs Nov. 2023 through Nov. 2025 with a dashed vertical line labeled 'Inauguration'; the y-axis is total employment in thousands (129,000-134,000). The chart shows the native-born employment level declining/flat under the blue Biden segment and rising under the red Trump segment after inauguration. A dark footer bar bears 'THE WHITE HOUSE WASHINGTON' and the round White House seal below. The technique uses a partisan-colored chart to credit the administration with rising native-born employment while blaming the prior administration. Tweet text claims 'The Biden administration CRUSHED the American Dream for American-born Workers... we're reversing this trend.' Target audience: the general public and Trump supporters.
notable_text: AMERICANS FIRST. | Total Native-Born Employment Level | Biden Administration / Trump Administration (so far) | Inauguration | THE WHITE HOUSE
